title: Panel – Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research? – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: An event panel announcement from the International Conference on Social Media & Society (#SMSociety 2026) listing Fabio Giglietto as an expert speaker. The roundtable addresses the transition from CrowdTangle to the Meta Content Library (MCL) and the subsequent hurdles for social media researchers.
description: An announcement for the #SMSociety conference panel featuring Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert speaker discussing the Meta Content Library and the future of social media research.

title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme
snippet: Conference program and schedule for the #SMSociety 2026 event in Glasgow, listing Fabio Giglietto as a prominent speaker and panelist on the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable.'
description: An announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist discussing future pathways for social media research following the closure of CrowdTangle.

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Media coverage outlining the public debate sparked by Rai3's 'Report' broadcast, which was heavily based on Fabio Giglietto's research paper, 'A Pretty Blunt Approach.' The article reviews the ongoing European Union transparency investigation into Meta's systemic risks following Giglietto's findings.
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: A detailed media report covering the findings of Fabio Giglietto's research, which analyzed over 2.5 million Facebook posts between 2021 and 2025. It details how Meta's political content reduction policies backfired, disproportionately suppressing institutional politicians while giving higher relative visibility to fringe, anti-establishment, and conspiracy-focused accounts.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: Media coverage of Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts to show how Meta's algorithm changes have heavily suppressed official political figures while boosting extremist and Eurosceptic groups.
description: Media coverage of a study by Fabio Giglietto and his team at the University of Urbino analyzing how Facebook's algorithm suppresses mainstream politics while boosting extremist content.
